result:
**Goal: Add 1D Elementary Cellular Automata (Wolfram rules) as a new simulation mode.**

This would render all 256 of Wolfram's elementary CA rules as scrolling space-time diagrams — each new generation appears as a row, with history scrolling upward to fill the terminal. The user would cycle through rules (Rule 30, Rule 110, Rule 90, etc.) or enter a rule number directly. Rule 110 is famously Turing-complete, Rule 30 is used as a PRNG, and Rule 90 produces Sierpinski triangles — all visually striking and fundamentally different from the 2D automata already in the project.

This is the natural complement to the existing 2D CA engine: a whole new class of automaton that's iconic, educational, and fills a clear gap in the simulator's coverage.
